_risk_level_doc: Two-file change: adds an optional CLI flag and updates a markdown spec. No shared infrastructure, no auth boundary, no schema change. OSError guard prevents grant consumption on bad path.

requirement.original: Systemically fix `execute-push.py` so it does not depend on the caller's current working directory (CWD) to determine which git repository to operate on.
requirement.clarified: Add optional `--repo-root` CLI parameter to `execute-push.py` (os.chdir at startup when provided; backward compat when absent). Update `commands/push.md` Step 2 to capture REPO_ROOT and Step 5 to pass --repo-root.
requirement.what: Add --repo-root optional arg to execute-push.py with os.chdir guard; update push.md Step 2 + Step 5 bash blocks
requirement.why: execute-push.py resolves git repo via CWD; if launched from wrong dir (e.g. nested .claude repo), git rev-parse HEAD returns wrong hash and drift check fails

included: scripts/execute-push.py — add --repo-root argparse arg, os.chdir call with OSError guard, docstring update

commands/push.md — Step 2 REPO_ROOT canonical capture via realpath, REPO_HASH derivation from ${REPO_ROOT} (no second realpath), Step 5 --repo-root argument in both invocation blocks (continuation line before --auto in auto block), removal of 'source venv/bin/activate &&' prefix from both Step 5 blocks
excluded: push-analyst grant format or validation logic — no changes
any other script or command file

success_criteria: execute-push.py accepts --repo-root; os.chdir fires before any git call when provided
Without --repo-root, behavior is identical to today
Invalid --repo-root causes exit 2 before any grant file is touched
push.md Step 2 captures REPO_ROOT as a standalone variable; REPO_HASH derives from it
push.md Step 5 passes --repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}" in both invocation blocks; --repo-root appears immediately before --auto in the auto block
push.md Step 5 invocation blocks use python3 directly (no 'source venv/bin/activate &&' prefix)
execute-push.py invoked from CWD /tmp with --repo-root /root reaches session_id check (error: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set, exit 2) rather than failing with a git-not-a-repository error
constraints: No changes to push-analyst grant format or validation logic
No changes to push-gate token format

root_cause_analysis.symptom: HEAD drift check fails when execute-push.py is launched from CWD that is a different git repo than the push target
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: execute-push.py line 220 calls subprocess.check_output(["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"]) without a cwd argument; Python subprocess inherits the process CWD, so git resolves whatever repo is rooted at or above that CWD
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: execute-push.py was written assuming it would always be invoked from within the correct repo root
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The orchestrator CWD can be the nested .claude repo (/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude) at Step 5 invocation time, making git resolve the .claude HEAD hash instead of the main repo HEAD hash
observations: scripts/execute-push.py:220 — subprocess.check_output(["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL) has no cwd= argument (inferred: static read)
commands/push.md:120 — REPO_HASH computed inline without capturing REPO_ROOT variable (inferred: static read)
commands/push.md:184-196 — Step 5 invocation blocks have no --repo-root argument (inferred: static read)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — symptom was observed at runtime by user but not reproduced in this session)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User provided verbatim error: grant.head_sha=2eecfa87 vs current HEAD=0e58363d. Static read of execute-push.py:220 confirms no cwd= on the subprocess call.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not triggered — this is a design omission, not a regression

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Add an optional `--repo-root` CLI parameter. When provided, `os.chdir()` to that path at startup (before any git operations).
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: execute-push.py argparse includes --repo-root (required=False, default=None); main() calls os.chdir(args.repo_root) before session_id check and before any git subprocess call when arg is not None; OSError guard exits 2 without touching grant files

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: When absent, behave as today (preserve backward compat for callers that already run from the correct CWD).
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: os.chdir is guarded by 'if args.repo_root is not None'; no behavior change when flag is omitted

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: update both [Step 2 and Step 5] to pass `--repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}"`
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: push.md Step 2 bash block contains 'REPO_ROOT=$(realpath "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")' as standalone line; REPO_HASH derives from ${REPO_ROOT} directly (no nested realpath on hash line); Step 5 non-auto and auto blocks each begin with 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' (no 'source venv/bin/activate &&' prefix) and each contain '--repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}"'; in the auto block --repo-root appears immediately before --auto

component_chain.role: Step 2 snapshot computation — defines REPO_ROOT and REPO_HASH shell variables for the push session
component_chain.evidence: push.md:120 REPO_HASH= line, push.md:184-196 Step 5 invocation (Read)

component_chain.role: CLI arg parsing — defines accepted parameters including the new --repo-root
main() — grant validation, HEAD drift check, sentinel write, os.execv push.sh

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Guards against git failure in HEAD drift check

setup.reason: non-UI -- CLI; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths. This is a CLI script fix invoked by the orchestrator bash step.

evidence.observed: HEAD drift error during /push: grant.head_sha=2eecfa87 but git rev-parse HEAD returned 0e58363d (nested .claude repo HEAD) because orchestrator CWD was /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude

expected.value: git rev-parse HEAD must resolve against the repo root captured at Step 2 (REPO_ROOT), not the process CWD at invocation time
expected.source: user requirement + static analysis of execute-push.py

evidence.gap: No CWD binding between Step 2 snapshot time and Step 5 script execution time

reference_source.description: Static read of scripts/execute-push.py and commands/push.md in this session

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts exist for this issue

context.recent_commits: e109534 docs(dev): update close-report-dev-20260524-205811; d23c483 feat(hooks): cross-SID grant cleanup

development_approach.strategy: Add optional --repo-root argparse argument to execute-push.py; insert os.chdir guard immediately after _parse_args() returns in main(); update module docstring with [--repo-root] in usage and exit code 2 for non-git path; replace REPO_HASH computation in push.md Step 2 with two canonical lines (REPO_ROOT=$(realpath ...) capture then REPO_HASH=${REPO_ROOT} derivation); remove 'source venv/bin/activate &&' from both Step 5 invocation blocks; add --repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}" continuation line in both blocks (immediately before --auto in the auto block).
development_approach.validation_approach: Static read: confirm --repo-root argparse entry (required=False, default=None); confirm os.chdir conditional before session_id check; confirm OSError guard with exit 2; confirm docstring update includes exit code 2 for non-git --repo-root; confirm push.md Step 2 has REPO_ROOT=$(realpath ...) as standalone line and REPO_HASH derives from ${REPO_ROOT} only; confirm both Step 5 blocks begin with 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' (no venv activation) and contain --repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}" with placement immediately before --auto in the auto block. Behavioral: invoke script from CWD /tmp with --repo-root /root and no CLAUDE_SESSION_ID; confirm error is 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set' (exit 2), NOT 'not a git repository'.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex validated the os.chdir() approach and raised 6 findings: (1) root cause framing too narrow — clarified that os.chdir covers push.sh CWD inheritance via os.execv; (2) REPO_HASH and REPO_ROOT should be computed from the same git call — incorporated; (3) OSError guard on invalid --repo-root path — incorporated with exit 2 before grant access; (4) docstring update needed — incorporated; (5) Step 2 itself run from wrong repo is out of scope — agreed, noted as design boundary; (6) backward compat ACs needed — incorporated.

findings.issue: Root cause framing too narrow — os.chdir covers push.sh CWD via os.execv, not just the single subprocess call
findings.proposed_fix: State invariant as: os.chdir before validation/drift/sentinel/os.execv so both execute-push.py and inherited push.sh use correct repo
findings.rationale: The spec already describes this correctly; Technical Hints section now makes it explicit

findings.issue: Step 2 should not compute REPO_HASH and REPO_ROOT via separate git rev-parse --show-toplevel calls
findings.rationale: Prevents hash/root mismatch if git rev-parse returns different values across two calls (e.g. CWD changes between calls). Incorporated into Must Have R3 and AC4.

findings.issue: No failure behavior defined for invalid --repo-root path
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap os.chdir in try/except OSError; print stderr; return 2 before any grant file access
findings.rationale: Without this guard, an invalid path causes an unhandled exception that crashes with a Python traceback instead of a clean error. Incorporated into Must Have R1 and AC2.

findings.issue: Module docstring Usage block would become stale
findings.proposed_fix: Add [--repo-root <REPO_ROOT>] to the usage line in the module docstring
findings.rationale: The docstring is the canonical usage reference for the script. AC6 added.

findings.issue: Change only freezes repo resolved during Step 2; does not solve Step 2 itself run from wrong repo
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit scope note: Step 2 must run from the intended repo; this fix freezes that snapshot for Step 5 execution
findings.rationale: Out of scope per user's hard bounds. Step 2 runs in the orchestrator's own context; that context is the user's responsibility. Noted in Non-Goals.

findings.issue: Draft lacks verification criteria for backward compatibility
findings.proposed_fix: Add AC: without --repo-root, no os.chdir occurs; behavior identical to today

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied findings 2, 3, 4, 6 (REPO_ROOT/REPO_HASH binding, OSError guard, docstring update, backward-compat AC). Finding 1 was already implicit; clarified in spec language. Finding 5 recorded as out of scope.
